How Escrow Works

Money moves only when you say so.

Every payment on TradeMatch is held in a regulated escrow account. The tradesperson gets paid only once you confirm the work is satisfactory — or after a mediation ruling. No exceptions.

Step 01
Payment lodged in escrow
When you accept a quote, the agreed amount is transferred to a regulated escrow account. It never touches the tradesperson.
Step 02
Work is completed
The tradesperson completes the job and submits a completion request with photographs and any certificates required.
Step 03
You review & confirm
You inspect the work and either approve it (releasing payment) or raise a concern within 72 hours of completion.
Step 04
Dispute raised (if needed)
If there's a problem, our mediation team steps in. Payment remains frozen until the dispute is resolved.
Step 05
Binding ruling issued
Our independent panel issues a binding outcome within 14 days. Payment is released or refunded accordingly.
Escrow — FCA Compliant
Why This Matters

No more paying upfront and hoping for the best.

FCA-regulated account
Funds are held under a Financial Conduct Authority regulated client account — not TradeMatch's general operating funds.
72-hour review window
You have 72 hours to inspect and confirm or raise a concern once the tradesperson submits completion.
Milestone payments supported
Large projects can be split into agreed milestones — each stage held and released independently.
Step-By-Step Process

From first concern to final resolution.

If you need to raise a dispute, here's exactly what happens — no hidden steps, no vague timelines. Every stage has a guaranteed deadline.

48h
First response SLA
8.3d
Avg resolution time
14d
Max binding timeline
£0
Cost to homeowner
1
Raise a concern in-app
Immediate
Tap "Raise Concern" on your job page. Describe the issue and upload photos or any supporting documents. The tradesperson is notified and payment is automatically frozen.
Escrow frozen instantly Tradesperson notified in-app & email Photo evidence uploaded
2
Direct resolution window
48 hours
The tradesperson has 48 hours to respond and attempt to resolve the issue directly. Many disputes are settled at this stage — a return visit to fix the snag, or an agreed partial refund.
In-app messaging channel opened Settlement agreement recorded digitally
3
Mediation team assigned
Within 72h
If direct resolution fails, a TradeMatch mediator is assigned within 72 hours. They review all evidence — photos, contracts, messaging history and completion records — independently.
Named mediator assigned Full case file reviewed Both parties may submit statements
4
Site inspection (if required)
Days 3–7
For complex cases — structural work, gas installations, electrical — an independent trade-qualified inspector may be dispatched to assess the work in person. TradeMatch bears the cost.
Trade-qualified inspector Full written inspection report No cost to homeowner
5
Binding ruling & payment action
By Day 14
The panel issues a binding ruling — full release, partial refund, full refund, or remedial work order. The escrow is settled accordingly. Both parties receive the written ruling with full reasoning.
Written ruling with full reasoning Escrow settled same-day Tradesperson rating updated
Independent — No favouritism
Trade-qualified inspectors

Tamper-proof record
Built-in Evidence System

Your case is built automatically.

Every conversation, document, photo and update on TradeMatch is automatically preserved in an immutable dispute record. You never need to scramble for screenshots or track down old emails.

Timestamped messaging history
Every message between you and the tradesperson is logged with timestamps. Cannot be altered or deleted by either party.
Geo-tagged completion photos
Tradesperson's completion photos are geo-tagged and date-stamped at capture — forgeries are impossible.
Original contract preserved
The agreed scope of work, price and timeline are locked at acceptance. No post-acceptance alterations permitted.
Cases resolved by panel
100%
No case left open
Independent Mediation Panel

Genuinely independent. No conflicts.

Our mediation panel is staffed by professionals with no financial relationship to TradeMatch. Former trading standards officers, chartered surveyors, and qualified trade inspectors review each case on its merits alone.

Rotating independent panel
Panel members are rotated to prevent any familiarity bias. Trade-specific cases matched to relevant expertise.
Binding on both parties
Rulings are contractually binding. Tradespeople who reject rulings are immediately suspended from the platform.
Full written reasoning
Every ruling comes with a plain-English explanation of the decision, with reference to the evidence considered.
Coverage

What the process covers.

Our dispute process is broad — but it helps to know what's in and what isn't so you can manage expectations from the start.

Covered by mediation
Work quality disputesThe finished work doesn't meet the agreed standard or a reasonable professional standard for the trade.
Incomplete workTradesperson abandoned the job or left agreed items unfinished without justification.
Scope creep disputesTradesperson charged for work outside the agreed scope without prior authorisation.
Property damage caused during worksDamage to your property directly caused by the tradesperson during the job.
Failure to return for snaggingTradesperson agreed to return for snags but failed to do so within a reasonable time.
Materials not as agreedCheaper or different materials used than those specified and agreed in the original quote.
Outside mediation scope
Change-of-mind disputesYou decided you don't like the style or colour after work was completed to specification.
Pre-existing conditionsDamage or issues that existed before the tradesperson arrived and were unrelated to their work.
Off-platform paymentsAny money paid directly to a tradesperson outside of the TradeMatch escrow system.
Verbal agreements not in contractExtras promised verbally but not added to the agreed written scope before work began.
Normal wear & settlementNatural material movement, shrinkage or minor cracking inherent to the materials used.
Late claims (over 90 days)Issues raised more than 90 days after the job was marked complete, with no prior notification.
Possible Outcomes

Three ways a dispute can be resolved.

Our panel selects the appropriate outcome based on the evidence. In most cases, a partial resolution — a return visit or partial refund — is the most proportionate result.

Remedial work order
Tradesperson returns to complete or fix specific items within an agreed timeframe. Escrow remains frozen until sign-off.
Most common
Partial or full refund
Escrow is split proportionately — tradesperson receives payment for acceptable work, homeowner refunded for the shortfall.
Fair split
Full release (tradesperson upheld)
Panel finds the work meets the agreed specification and professional standard. Full payment released to tradesperson.
Trade upheld
98.7%
of disputes fully resolved without court action

24%

Zero tolerance policy
Consequences Are Real

A ruling against you has consequences.

TradeMatch isn't just a booking platform — it's a quality-enforced marketplace. Tradespeople who lose disputes face automatic consequences that protect every future homeowner too.

Dispute outcome on public profile
Resolved disputes are noted on the tradesperson's public profile — future homeowners can see the full resolution history.
Escrow withheld until compliance
If remedial work is ordered, the full escrow remains frozen until the homeowner confirms the remediation is complete.
Repeat offenders suspended or banned
Three upheld disputes in 24 months results in automatic suspension pending review. Two bans in five years: permanent removal.
Common Questions

Everything you need to know.

If your question isn't answered here, our disputes team is available 9am–6pm Monday to Friday.

Disputes team
Available Mon–Fri · 9am–6pm
How do I raise a dispute on TradeMatch?
Go to the job page in your account, tap "Raise a Concern" and describe the issue. Upload any photos or documents. The tradesperson is notified immediately and the escrow is frozen. You'll hear from our team within 48 hours.
Is there a deadline for raising a dispute?
You must raise any concern within 72 hours of confirming job completion, or within 90 days of the job being marked complete if a defect only becomes apparent later. We strongly recommend raising issues as soon as they're discovered.
What if the tradesperson just ignores the dispute?
If the tradesperson fails to respond within the 48-hour direct resolution window, the case is automatically escalated to our mediation team. Non-response is considered a concession and the panel will likely rule in your favour. The tradesperson cannot prevent a ruling by going silent.
Does mediation cost me anything?
No — mediation is completely free for homeowners. Any site inspection costs are borne by TradeMatch. If the dispute is upheld in your favour, those costs may be recovered from the tradesperson's account.
Can I still go to court if I'm unhappy with the ruling?
Yes. Our mediation process is designed to resolve disputes without court action, but it doesn't prevent you from pursuing your legal rights. The panel ruling and all evidence records can be used in court proceedings. However, 98.7% of cases are resolved without requiring further legal action.
What happens to my money while a dispute is ongoing?
Your payment remains frozen in the FCA-regulated escrow account for the entire duration of the dispute. It cannot be released to the tradesperson without your approval or a panel ruling. The funds are completely safe throughout the process.
Can disputes affect a tradesperson's future work on TradeMatch?
Yes. Upheld disputes are recorded on the tradesperson's profile. Three upheld disputes in a 24-month window triggers automatic suspension pending review. This accountability system ensures poor performers are removed before they can cause more harm.
